message:
fix(tui): update keymap test fixtures for yank binding and conflict detection

Two test adjustments in tui_keymap.rs:

- detect_conflicts_no_overlap: Changed test fixture from ("y", "Do Y")
  to ("z", "Do Z") because "y" is now assigned to the yank/clipboard
  action (added in the recent compose modal + clipboard yank feature).
  Using "y" in a no-overlap test would conflict with the global binding.

- text_suppressible exhaustiveness: Added "y" to the list of bindings
  that should be text-suppressible, since the yank binding must not
  interfere with text input in compose/search modes.

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with latest concurrent agent work
tracking TUI accessibility, performance HUD, and search improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"br-2e9jp.4","title":"Phase 4: WASM Web Mode - Browser-Based Agent Mail Dashboard","description":"# Phase 4: WASM Web Mode - Browser-Based Agent Mail Dashboard

## Objective
Compile the TUI to WebAssembly for browser-based access to Agent Mail state.

## Architecture

Browser (WASM TUI with Canvas Terminal via frankenterm-web) <-> WebSocket <-> MCP Agent Mail Server (State Sync Service with delta compression)

## Key Deliverables

- T4.1: WASM Build Target - Configure Cargo for wasm32-unknown-unknown
- T4.2: Web Frontend Shell - HTML/JS shell with Canvas terminal
- T4.3: WebSocket State Sync - Delta-compressed real-time updates
- T4.4: Cloudflare Pages Deployment - Static hosting with global CDN

## Benefits

1. Remote monitoring: View agent mail state from any device
2. No installation: Just open a URL
3. Share state: Send links to specific views
4. Mobile access: Works on tablets/phones

## Performance Targets

- WASM bundle size: <2MB gzipped
- Initial load time: <3s on 3G
- Frame time: <33ms (30 FPS)
- WebSocket latency: <100ms

## Dependencies
Requires Phase 3 (Visual Polish) completion. Theme system must work in browser.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:

TUI runtime now drains remote ingress queue on ticks and routes events through the existing terminal reducer path. README transport contract updated accordingly.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:11:03Z"}]}

Added tests for snapshot/delta/message application. Validation passed: cargo check --all-targets, cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown, cargo test, cargo clippy -D warnings (all for mcp-agent-mail-wasm). Remaining gap: server-side /ws state publisher path in mcp-agent-mail-server is still needed for full bead completion.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:01:24Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-edpom","title":"T15.1: Unit tests for Mermaid syntax generation and form validation","description":"Unit tests for features not covered by per-track test beads:

MERMAID GENERATION TESTS (T4.1 output verification):
- Contact graph Mermaid syntax has correct node/edge format
- Thread flow sequence diagram has correct participant/message format
- System overview Mermaid syntax validates against Mermaid grammar
- Edge labels include correct message counts
- Empty data produces valid empty graph
- 50-node graph generates in < 10ms

FORM VALIDATION TESTS (T9.1 validation logic):
- Agent name autocomplete matches registered agents
- Subject field required (rejects empty)
- Subject field max length 200 chars (rejects 201+)
- Body field required (rejects empty)
- To field validates against known agent names
- CC field accepts comma-separated agent names
- Thread ID auto-generation produces valid format
- Importance select defaults to Normal
- Ack Required checkbox defaults to false

INTEGRATION TEST:
- Form submission constructs correct MCP tool parameter JSON
- Form submission dispatches to send_message tool
- Form error propagates to toast notification

Target: 15+ tests.

 {"id":"br-ekg2h","title":"T13.2: Implement keyboard drag-and-drop variant","description":"Keyboard-accessible alternative to mouse DnD:
1. Ctrl+M on selected item -> mark for move (visual indicator: dashed border)
2. Navigate to destination (different thread, different screen even)
3. Ctrl+V -> execute drop action
4. Escape -> cancel marked item

Only one item can be marked at a time. Marking a new item cancels the previous mark.

Visual indicator: marked item gets a dashed border and "[MOVING]" badge.

FILES: tui_app.rs (marked_item state), tui_screens/messages.rs, tui_screens/threads.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Ctrl+M marks selected item with visual indicator
- [ ] Ctrl+V executes move to current context
- [ ] Escape cancels mark
- [ ] Only one item marked at a time
- [ ] Works across screen switches (mark on Messages, drop on Threads)

-{"id":"br-eoxga","title":"T8.3: Unit tests for focus graph and spatial navigation","description":"Test focus graph construction and spatial navigation logic.

TEST CASES:

Focus graph construction:
- Dashboard with 4 panels produces 4-node focus graph
- Spatial neighbors computed correctly from panel Rects
- Right neighbor of leftmost panel is next panel to the right
- Down neighbor of top panel is panel below
- Panels that don't overlap on any axis have no neighbor in that direction

Spatial navigation:
- Ctrl+Right from left panel moves focus to right panel
- Ctrl+Down from top panel moves focus to bottom panel
- Ctrl+Right on rightmost panel wraps or does nothing (configurable)
- Tab cycles through all panels in document order
- Shift+Tab cycles in reverse order

Focus memory:
- Focus state saved on screen exit
- Focus state restored on screen entry
- Default focus for new screen is first panel

Focus traps:
- Modal captures focus (Tab stays within modal)
- Dismissing modal restores previous focus

Target: 15+ tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 5+ focus graph construction tests
- [ ] 5+ spatial navigation tests
- [ ] 3+ focus memory tests
- [ ] 2+ focus trap tests
- [ ] Tests verify focus graph matches panel layout

 {"id":"br-erlj","title":"T4.5: Tests for check-inbox (rate limiting, HTTP path, direct DB path)","description":"## Objective
Build thorough unit/integration coverage for check-inbox behavior across rate limiting, transport modes, and failure handling.

## Work
- Test polling decisions, lock behavior, and anti-spam semantics.
- Validate remote JSON-RPC and direct DB mode consistency.
- Assert deterministic diagnostics and output shape across edge and failure paths.

## Deliverable
A test suite that makes inbox-check behavior dependable under real workflow pressure.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:58.283776884Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:53:04.041252731Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:53:04.041232944Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-erlj","depends_on_id":"br-y999","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:24.352936297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":215,"issue_id":"br-erlj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T4.5: Tests for check-inbox

## What to test

### Unit tests
1. **Rate limiter**: Verify that two calls within 30s → second returns false
2. **Rate limiter after interval**: Verify that call after 30s+ → returns true
3. **Rate limiter concurrency**: Two threads calling simultaneously → only one proceeds
4. **Rate limiter disabled**: interval=0 → always returns true
5. **InboxCheckResult formatting**: Verify human-readable output for 0, 1, N unread

### Integration tests (direct DB path)
6. **Direct query with seeded DB**: Create DB, insert messages, verify correct count
7. **Direct query empty inbox**: No unread messages → count=0
8. **Direct query nonexistent agent**: Returns 0 unread (not an error)
9. **Direct query missing DB**: Clear error message

### Integration tests (HTTP path)
10. **HTTP error handling**: Connection refused → clear error message
11. **HTTP auth error**: 401 → "check HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN" message

### CLI tests
12. **Flag parsing**: Verify all flags accepted
13. **--json output**: Verify JSON has expected fields

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/check_inbox.rs (mod tests)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/check_inbox_integration.rs

 {"id":"br-f5qmi","title":"R2.4: Implement am robot overview — cross-project unified summary (per-project unread/urgent/ack counts)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:58.413098756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:06.462283437Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:06.462216622Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-f5qmi","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:47.236115858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":306,"issue_id":"br-f5qmi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R2.4: `am robot overview`

## What
Cross-project aggregation. When an agent works in a multi-project environment, this shows a unified view of all projects with per-project counts.

## Data Collection
For each project in the database:
1. Query unread message count (for current agent, or all if agent unknown)
2. Query urgent message count
3. Query ack-overdue count (ack_required AND ack_ts IS NULL AND created_at < 30min ago)
4. Query active reservations count

## SQL (per project)
```sql
-- Unread count
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages WHERE project_id = ? AND to_agent = ? AND read_at IS NULL;

-- Urgent count
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages WHERE project_id = ? AND to_agent = ? AND read_at IS NULL AND importance = 'urgent';

-- Ack-overdue count
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages WHERE project_id = ? AND to_agent = ? AND ack_required = 1 AND ack_ts IS NULL AND created_at < ?;

-- Active reservations
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM file_reservations WHERE project_id = ? AND expires_at > ?;
```

Can be optimized into a single query with CASE/SUM per project.

## Output Format
```
projects[3]{slug,unread,urgent,ack_overdue,reservations}:
  backend-api,5,1,0,3
  frontend-app,2,0,1,2
  shared-lib,0,0,0,0

total_unread: 7
total_urgent: 1
total_ack_overdue: 1

_alerts[1]{severity,summary}:
  warn,1 ack-overdue message in frontend-app

_actions[1]:
  am robot inbox --project frontend-app --ack-overdue
```

## Agent Identity
- If agent is known: filter messages to that agent's inbox
- If agent unknown: show total counts across all agents
- Add _alert if agent not detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lists all projects in database
- Per-project counts are accurate
- Totals match sum of per-project counts
- _alerts generated for projects with ack-overdue items
- Works with 0 projects (empty table, clean output)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-g7z2m","title":"Track G: BOCPD + Conformal Prediction for Tool Metrics","description":"**Background**

The tool metrics system currently uses static thresholds for alerting. BOCPD (Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection) can detect regime shifts in tool latency/throughput, and Conformal Prediction provides distribution-free prediction intervals for SLO monitoring.

**Graveyard references:** Section 12.13 (BOCPD), Section 12.1 (Conformal Prediction).

**EV calculation (BOCPD):** (severity=4 * breadth=4 * feasibility=3) / (risk=3 * effort=2) = 8.0
**EV calculation (Conformal):** (severity=4 * breadth=4 * feasibility=4) / (risk=3 * effort=2) = 10.7","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- BOCPD change-point detector implemented with configurable hazard and stable numerics
- Conformal prediction intervals implemented for SLO monitoring with calibration safeguards
- Wired into tool metrics screen and TUI display with clear operator semantics
- Unit tests cover detector updates, interval construction, and edge-case calibration failures
- Integration tests validate detector + conformal interoperability over streaming tool metrics
- E2E scenarios validate visible anomalies/intervals under synthetic incident injections
- Bench artifacts measure per-update overhead and memory growth under sustained traffic
- Detailed logging includes run-length/posterior summaries, interval bounds, and alert trigger rationale","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:42:47.967784178Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:42:47.967714698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-g7z2m","depends_on_id":"br-194ry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:14.739242493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-g7z2m","depends_on_id":"br-347am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:14.157721940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-g7z2m","depends_on_id":"br-h8xsy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:49:15.291980090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-gk8vs","title":"T15.4: E2E test script for data visualization (charts, canvas, heatmap)","description":"Create tests/e2e/test_tui_v3_charts.sh testing chart widgets end-to-end.

TEST APPROACH:
- Start server with TUI_ENABLED=true and known test data
- Send synthetic events via HTTP to populate EventRingBuffer
- Capture TUI frame buffer via ftui-harness headless mode
- Assert that chart areas contain expected visual patterns

TEST CASES:
1. Dashboard LineChart area is non-empty after sending 10 ToolCallEnd events
2. ToolMetrics BarChart area shows bars after sending events for 3 different tools
3. Dashboard Canvas heatmap area has colored dots after sending mixed event types
4. Charts scale correctly on 80x24 terminal (compact mode)
5. Charts scale correctly on 200x50 terminal (wide mode)
6. Charts degrade gracefully when no data available (empty state)
7. Chart transitions are smooth (no visual glitches between frames)

LOGGING:
- Log each synthetic event sent (type, timestamp, tool name)
- Log frame capture dimensions and non-empty cell counts per chart area
- Log any assertion failures with full frame buffer dump
- Log test timing for performance regression detection

 {"id":"br-gtdw2","title":"[track] T5: Tree Widget for Thread Hierarchy Navigation","description":"Replace flat thread lists with a hierarchical Tree widget that shows reply chains as
expandable/collapsible branches. This dramatically improves thread navigation for
complex multi-agent conversations.

FRANKENTUI TREE WIDGET:
- ftui_widgets::tree::Tree — Hierarchical data display
- 5 guide styles: Plain, Rounded, Bold, Double, ASCII
- Expandable/collapsible nodes
- Keyboard navigation: up/down/left(collapse)/right(expand)/space(toggle)
- Custom node rendering via TreeItem trait

DESIGN:
Thread tree structure:
```
▼ [br-123] Feature: Add authentication (5 msgs)
  ├── GoldHawk: Start implementation [2m ago]
  │   ├── SilverFox: Re: Progress update [1m ago]
  │   │   └── GoldHawk: Re: Re: Completed [30s ago]
  │   └── CoralBadger: Re: Code review [45s ago]
  └── RubyPrairie: Summary [10s ago]
```

Each node shows: sender name, subject/snippet, relative time, read/unread status.
Expand/collapse to manage visual complexity in large threads.

FILES: tui_screens/threads.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Tree renders with reply hierarchy
- [ ] Expand/collapse with arrow keys and space
- [ ] Guide style matches theme (Rounded by default)
- [ ] Unread messages highlighted
- [ ] Sender names color-coded by agent
- [ ] Node selection navigates to message in preview pane
- [ ] Works with threads containing 100+ messages

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:33:12.173746692Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","threads","tree-widget","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-gtdw2","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:57.175132250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":586,"issue_id":"br-gtdw2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN RATIONALE (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

WHY A TREE WIDGET FOR THREADS:

Current thread display is a flat list of messages. When agent conversations are deep
(10+ messages with nested replies), it's extremely difficult to understand the reply
structure and find specific sub-conversations.

The frankentui Tree widget with 5 guide styles (Plain, Rounded, Bold, Double, ASCII) provides
exactly the hierarchical navigation needed. Key advantages:
1. Visual reply depth at a glance (indentation + guides)
2. Collapse old branches to focus on recent activity
3. Expand specific sub-conversations without losing context
4. Natural keyboard navigation (Left=collapse, Right=expand)

TREE CONSTRUCTION FROM FLAT MESSAGES:

Messages in Agent Mail have:
- message_id: unique ID
- reply_to_id: optional, references parent message
- thread_id: groups messages into threads

Algorithm:
1. Collect all messages in thread
2. Build parent->children map using reply_to_id
3. Messages with no reply_to_id are root nodes
4. Sort children by created_at ascending
5. Build recursive TreeItem structure

Edge cases:
- reply_to_id references non-existent message: treat as root
- Circular references (shouldn't happen but): detect and break cycle
- Very deep nesting (> 20 levels): flatten beyond depth 20

GUIDE STYLE: Use Rounded by default (matches BorderType::Rounded used throughout the TUI).
Allow override via env var AM_TUI_TREE_STYLE=rounded|plain|bold|double|ascii.

INTEGRATION WITH EXISTING THREADS SCREEN:
The Threads screen currently has a list pane and a preview pane. Replace the list pane
with the Tree widget. Selection in the tree updates the preview pane. This is a drop-in
replacement that preserves the existing UX while adding hierarchical navigation.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:10:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-h1yu","title":"Track 2: am flake-triage — Native flake analysis (replaces scripts/flake_triage.sh)","description":"## Purpose
Replace `scripts/flake_triage.sh` with native `am flake-triage` subcommands for scan/reproduce/detect workflows using typed Rust logic instead of inline Python parsing.

## Scope
- Artifact scanning and parsing.
- Failure reproduction from artifact metadata.
- Multi-seed flake detection with deterministic run metadata.
- CLI UX and JSON output parity for automation.
- Controlled script deprecation after native verification.

## Why this matters
Flake diagnosis is high-friction by default; native tooling should make diagnosis faster, reproducible, and easier to automate.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Unit + integration + e2e tests include positive/negative cases and artifact corruption/edge handling.
- Logs are rich enough to diagnose flaky vs hard failures without manual script inspection.
- Legacy script path is marked compatibility/deprecated after native test evidence is in place.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:49.458275594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:59.909019587Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:59.908999880Z","close_reason":"Track complete: all child tasks closed (br-36xx artifact scanning, br-1kk7 reproduction, br-154k multi-seed detection, br-32ax CLI wiring, br-shfc unit tests, br-1oh3n E2E tests, br-137e deprecation notice). Native 'am flake-triage' fully replaces scripts/flake_triage.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-h1yu","depends_on_id":"br-137e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:37:16.920649034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"br-h1yu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 2: am flake-triage — Native Flake Analysis

## What it replaces
scripts/flake_triage.sh (194 lines of bash)

## Current behavior
Three modes of operation:
1. **reproduce**: Re-run a specific test from a failure artifact JSON file
2. **scan**: Walk directory trees to find failure artifact files, summarize them
3. **detect**: Multi-seed flake detection — run a test N times with different seeds,
   report if it passes/fails intermittently (true flake vs hard failure)

All JSON parsing uses inline Python one-liners like:
  python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$file')); print(d.get('test_name',''))"

## External dependencies eliminated
- **python3**: Every JSON operation uses an inline Python script. Rust's serde_json
  replaces this entirely.

## Key improvements
1. **Existing Rust module**: crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs already has
   domain logic for flake analysis. The bash script is a less-capable duplicate. This
   track wires the existing module into the CLI.
2. **Structured output**: Instead of printing text, produce JSON with test names,
   failure rates, seed values, and reproduction commands.
3. **Parallel multi-seed**: The detect mode can run N seeds in parallel using
   std::process::Command with thread pooling, much faster than bash's sequential loop.
4. **Cross-platform**: No bash-specific path handling or process management.

## CLI interface
```
am flake-triage scan [--dir <path>] [--json]
am flake-triage reproduce <artifact.json> [--json]
am flake-triage detect <test_name> [--seeds N] [--parallel] [--json]
```

## Implementation location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/flake_triage.rs (new module, wraps core::flake_triage)
Wire into Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

## Files to read for context
- scripts/flake_triage.sh (the script being replaced)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs (existing Rust module to wrap)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (CLI structure to extend)
